jobs.title: President & Chief Operating Officer
jobs.description:
Why Churchstreeter and Crosse:
Churchstreeter and Crosse is redefining healthcare consulting by combining executive operational expertise, workforce strategy, and advanced AI technology into one integrated partnership model. Our executive leaders work alongside healthcare organizations—not as outside consultants, but as trusted strategic partners committed to measurable outcomes.

In this position...
. We are seeking an accomplished healthcare executive to serve as President. This executive will partner directly with the Chief Executive Officer to lead enterprise operations, client delivery, growth strategy, and organizational performance while ensuring the exceptional execution of all consulting, talent, technology, and operational services. This is not a traditional corporate operations position. The President serves as both an executive consultant and operational leader, partnering directly with C-suite executives across the country to solve complex workforce and operational challenges

Job Summary

The President will be responsible for transforming strategy into execution across the organization while serving as the executive sponsor for health system partnerships. This leader must possess deep operational credibility within large integrated healthcare systems and have successfully led complex, multi-site organizations through transformation, growth, workforce stabilization, operational improvement, mergers, acquisitions, or system integration.
The President serves as both an executive consultant and operational leader, partnering directly with C-suite executives across the country to solve complex workforce and operational challenges.

Key Responsibilities

Executive Leadership
: Partner with the CEO to develop and execute the organization's strategic vision, oversee enterprise-wide operations, drive operational excellence and organizational performance, ensure achievement of financial and client success metrics, build scalable business processes, and develop leadership succession plans to support long-term growth.
Client Executive Leadership
: Serve as the executive sponsor for strategic healthcare partnerships by building trusted relationships with executive leaders and boards, providing consultative guidance on healthcare operations, workforce strategy, and organizational transformation, while leading executive governance meetings and presenting strategic recommendations that drive client success and organizational performance.
Operational Consulting
: Provide executive oversight for healthcare consulting engagements by leading strategic initiatives in healthcare operations, workforce transformation, talent acquisition and retention, organizational development, service line optimization, change management, executive coaching, performance improvement, and workforce analytics to achieve measurable operational and organizational outcomes.
Business Operations
: Oversee consulting delivery teams to ensure high-quality, evidence-based client engagements, collaborate with technology leaders to advance AI and digital workforce initiatives, monitor client satisfaction and executive relationships, and drive continuous improvement across consulting methodologies and operational processes.
Business Development:
Support business growth by contributing to executive sales presentations, strategic proposal development, and expansion initiatives, while representing Churchstreeter and Crosse at executive meetings, industry conferences, and events to strengthen client relationships and expand market presence.

Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree required. Master’s degree is preferred.
Minimum 15 years of progressive healthcare leadership experience.
Minimum 10 years in executive healthcare operations.
Prior experience as one or more of the following IS A MUST: President, Chief Operating Officer (COO), Senior Vice President of Operations, Vice President of Operations, Regional COO, or Division COO
Experience within large integrated healthcare systems
Demonstrated responsibility for multi-hospital operations
Experience leading large-scale organizational transformation
Strong preference for candidates possessing one or more of the following: Registered Nurse (RN), Clinical leadership background, Human Resources executive experience, Chief Human Resources Officer experience, Talent acquisition transformation experience, or Consulting leadership experience
Experience working with: Academic Medical Centers, Integrated Delivery Networks, Multi-state Health Systems, Children's Hospitals, Faith-based Healthcare Organizations, or Large Nonprofit Health Systems

Work Environment

This role typically operates in a hybrid environment depending on organizational needs. Occasional travel may be required for industry events, networking, or candidate/client engagement.

jobs.title: Director of Development - Power
jobs.description:
Director of Power Development

Location
: United States (remote with travel as needed)
Overview

We are seeking a senior power development leader to build and lead a U.S. power project pipeline from the ground up- a true originator with proven experience in the utility space. This role focuses on originating, structuring, and advancing large-scale energy infrastructure projects, particularly grid-scale storage and generation.
This is not an operations, engineering, or plant management role.
This is a front-end origination and commercial leadership position responsible for creating and advancing revenue-generating opportunities.
The ideal candidate is a strategic deal maker who understands U.S. power markets, can negotiate complex commercial agreements, and has successfully advanced projects to financial close.

Key Responsibilities

• Build and lead a U.S. power development pipeline from origination through commercial maturity
• Identify and originate grid-scale storage and generation opportunities
• Structure and negotiate Power Purchase Agreements, Letters of Intent, and other offtake agreements
• Advance projects within ISO/RTO markets such as ERCOT, PJM, CAISO, MISO, and ISO-NE
• Evaluate pricing dynamics, merchant risk, and capacity market exposure
• Develop and maintain relationships with utilities, municipalities, and large power buyers
• Structure early-stage commercial frameworks to move projects toward investment readiness
• Collaborate with internal investment, finance, and execution teams to progress projects toward close

*Required Qualifications*

• 10 to 15+ years of experience in U.S. power project development
• Direct experience operating within ISO/RTO market structures
• Proven track record of negotiating and closing multiple PPAs
• Experience developing grid-scale storage and/or generation projects
• Strong network within utilities, municipalities, and large commercial power buyers
• Demonstrated ability to originate and structure early-stage energy infrastructure deals

Preferred Experience

• Infrastructure concession or public-private partnership experience
• Experience structuring power supply solutions for data centers or hyperscalers
• District energy project exposure
• Track record advancing 100MW+ projects to commercial viability

Who This Role Is For

This role is ideal for a senior development professional from an Independent Power Producer, infrastructure developer, or grid-scale storage company who has led commercial negotiations and built project pipelines.
It is not suited for candidates whose background is primarily in operations, engineering, transmission, or EPC construction without commercial origination experience.

jobs.title: Development Director, St. Luke, Shoreline
jobs.description:
St. Luke School is enthusiastically seeking a Development Director starting when the position is filled. This is a full-time position.

School Overview

Tradition meets innovation at St. Luke School, where we honor our rich history and traditions, embrace the present, and continue always to move forward. Founded in 1957, St. Luke School serves students from age three through eighth grade. St. Luke Primary Years (PYP) and Middle Years (MYP) offer co-ed programs in Preschool through Grade 8, welcoming students from around the world. We are a fully authorized International Baccalaureate World School delivering IB programming to all our students. St. Luke Mission Statement: Through inquiry and reflection, we educate globally- minded citizens who live the love of Jesus. We are inquiring minds, hearts for Jesus, and hands for service.

St. Luke School seeks a dynamic, relationship-driven Advancement Director to lead the school's fundraising, community engagement, and philanthropic initiatives. As a member of the school's leadership team, the Advancement Director serves as the chief architect of advancement efforts, fostering a culture of philanthropy while building meaningful relationships with donors, families, alumni, parishioners, volunteers, sponsors, and community partners.

Working closely with the Principal, the Advancement Director is responsible for developing and implementing strategies that support the long-term financial sustainability and mission of the school through fundraising, donor stewardship, community partnerships, special events, advancement communications, and strategic engagement initiatives. The Advancement Director should have a strong understanding of Catholic values, traditions, and educational principles. The Advancement Director plays a key role in strengthening relationships throughout the St. Luke community while helping advance collaborative opportunities that support the shared mission and long-term success of St. Luke School and its partnership with Christ the King.

Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in nonprofit management, business administration, marketing, communications, education, or a related field.
Minimum of five years of progressively responsible experience in fundraising, advancement, nonprofit leadership, education, marketing, communications, or a related field.
Demonstrated success in fundraising, donor relations, event management, sponsorship development, community engagement, or related work.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to engage, inspire, and build relationships with diverse stakeholders.
Experience working collaboratively with volunteers, committees, boards, and leadership teams.
Proficiency in donor management software, Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, and related technology platforms. (Finalsite Enrollment, Little Green Light, Greater Giving, Vanco)
Ability to work occasional evenings and weekends as required by events and school activities.

Salary And Benefits

Salary range:
$75,000-85,000 per year. Any offered salary is determined based on internal equity, internal salary ranges, market data, applicant's skills and prior relevant experience, degrees, and certifications.

Benefits: A comprehensive package of benefits is offered including two medical plan options, dental, vision, life, accidental death and disability, long term disability, pension, 403(b), HSA, FSA, and generous sick leave, vacation, and holidays. For staff in the first 5 years of their careers, we offer the opportunity to apply for affordable, innovative housing options, based on availability.

Application process

St. Luke School remains firmly committed to a policy of equal employment opportunity. St Luke School is an equal opportunity employer following the employment guidelines of the Archdiocese of Seattle. Applicants of diverse and underrepresented backgrounds are encouraged to apply.

To apply, please complete an application in frontline and attach a resume, cover letter, and 3-5 professional references, all in PDF format, ATTN Maureen Berg, principal at St. Luke School. Upon review of the application, qualified candidates will be contacted for an interview at the school. Position will be open until filled.

jobs.title: Deputy Chief Operating Officer
jobs.description:
DEPUTY CHIEF OPERATING OFFICER

Lafayette Federal Credit Union

$2.1B Assets
Rockville, MD

Are We the Place for You?

Lafayette Federal Credit Union (LFCU), headquartered in Rockville, Maryland, is seeking a Deputy Chief Operating Officer (DCOO) to serve as a designated partner to the Chief Operating Officer. For more than 90 years, LFCU has supported over 57,000 members and manages more than $2.1 billion in assets, earning a strong reputation for financial strength, personalized service, and trusted member relationships.

LFCU is built on a culture of collaboration, integrity, and service excellence, with a strong focus on empowering members, strengthening communities, and creating an environment where employees can make a meaningful impact.

Our Mission Statement:

"We SERVE, SUPPORT, and EMPOWER our members by understanding their financial needs; delivering products and services to achieve their financial goals and offering solutions to assure their financial well-being."

Our Vision Statement:

"Your credit union for life."

This vision reflects our commitment to being a lifelong trusted financial partner, delivering exceptional experience and innovative solutions at every stage of our members’ financial journey.

Is This the Career for You?

The Deputy Chief Operating Officer serves as a strategic support partner to the COO, focused on strengthening execution, coordination, and follow-through across key operational priorities. This role helps ensure that enterprise initiatives move forward efficiently by organizing decision support materials, tracking commitments, and maintaining visibility into project status, risks, and dependencies. Acting as an extension of the COO when delegated, the Deputy COO facilitates communication across executive leaders and operational teams to keep priorities aligned and on schedule, without directly managing senior leaders or their departments.

In addition to day-to-day operational coordination, the role plays a key part in managing cross-functional initiatives such as process improvements, space planning, branch expansion, mergers, and enterprise projects. The Deputy COO also supports regulatory readiness by coordinating exam preparation, audit follow-ups, and documentation across internal stakeholders and external regulators.

Overall, this position is best suited for someone who thrives in structured execution, executive-level coordination, and enterprise project management, serving as a connector who turns strategy into action through organization, influence, and disciplined follow-through.

Help Us Get to Know You?

Bachelor’s degree in business administration, Finance, Management, or a related field is required, with an MBA or advanced degree preferred. The role requires 8–12 years of progressive experience in financial services, banking, credit unions, operations, project management, or similar leadership support roles. Candidates should demonstrate strong experience coordinating across multiple operational functions, including lending, servicing, branch operations, facilities, information technology, project management, and enterprise-wide strategic initiatives.

Compensation:
The starting salary range for this position is $170,000 to $220,000 annually.

ABOUT DDJ MYERS, an ALM First Company

DDJ Myers is the executive search consultant for this organization. Our expertise is in supporting leadership teams in sustainable practices for high-performing organizations through succession planning, leadership development programs, executive coaching, board governance and renewal, and executive search. Please learn more about us at www.ddjmyers.com .

jobs.title: DIRECTOR OF MAJOR GIFTS
jobs.description:
Summary

JOB SUMMARY

Overall responsibility for initiating, coordinating, and managing major gift fundraising activities to attract and gain philanthropic support for Carroll Hospital Center and BridgingLife. Plan, organize, and create opportunities for individuals to make major and/or planned gifts for the benefit of Carroll Hospital Center and BridgingLife.

Responsibilities

Responsible for identifying, cultivating, soliciting and stewarding a portfolio of at least 100 major and/or planned gifts, individual, corporate or foundation prospects.
Works with the Vice President of Development to annually formulate a strategic plan and budget for the Foundation and provide timely and accurate updates on progress towards the goals each year.
Develop the volunteer structure needed to raise funds and work with leadership to conduct 1-1 major and planned giving solicitations.
Serve as lead staff to work with board and volunteer committees to conduct prospect review and grow the pipeline of major supporters.
Maintain relations with existing donors including conducting renewal and stewardship of Founder Circle level donors of $1,000 or more and support development of new donors.
Serve as lead for patient rounding and ensuring appropriate stewardship during hospital experience.
Develops and sustains excellent relationships in the community in order to advance and maintain health care at the highest level at the hospital, hospice and its subsidiaries.
Manage the donor relations process for major gifts ($10,000 +) and ensure timely and personalized donor acknowledgment, including written and verbal recognition.

Requirements

3-5+ years of relevant experience in philanthropy, development, and major gift fundraising, preferrably in healthcare
Bachelor's degree preferred

Additional Information

Who We Are:

LifeBridge Health is a dynamic, purpose-driven health system redefining care delivery across the mid-Atlantic and beyond, anchored by our mission to “improve the health of people in the communities we serve.” Join us to advance health access, elevate patient experiences, and contribute to a system that values bold ideas and community-centered care.

What We Offer

Impact: Join a team that values innovation and outcomes, delivering life-saving care to our youngest and most vulnerable patients.

Growth : Opportunities for professional development, including tuition reimbursement and developing foundational skills for neonatal critical care leadership and advanced certification.

Support: A culture of collaboration with resources like unit-based practice councils and advanced clinical education support — improving both workflow efficiency and patient outcomes and allowing you to work at the top of your license.

Benefits : Competitive compensation (additional compensation such as overtime, shift differentials, premium pay, and bonuses may apply depending on job), comprehensive health plans, free parking, and wellness programs.

Why LifeBridge Health?

With over 14,000 employees, 130 care locations, and two million annual patient encounters, we combine strategic growth, innovation, and deep community commitment to deliver exceptional care anchored by five leading centers in the Baltimore region: Sinai Hospital of Baltimore, Grace Medical Center, Northwest Hospital, Carroll Hospital, and Levindale Hebrew Geriatric Center and Hospital.

Our organization thrives on a culture of CARE BRAVELY—where compassion, courage, and urgency drive every decision, empowering teams to shape the future of healthcare.

LifeBridge Health complies with applicable Federal civil rights laws and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, age, disability, sex or sexual orientation and gender identity/expression. LifeBridge Health does not exclude people or treat them differently because of race, color, national origin, age, disability, sex or sexual orientation and gender identity/expression.

jobs.description:
Chief Executive Officer | Recycling and/or waste operations | Virginia

MUST HAVES

Recycling and/or waste operations, environmental sectors preferred (will consider industries such as industrial, logistics, warehousing, and/or manufacturing overseeing multi-site/multi-location.
10+ years experience in progressive executive leadership role
Direct P&L $50m
Proven experience leading business through scale up, restructuring, and/or turnaround situations.

The Company:

Operating through a global joint venture partnership, our client is rapidly growing recycling business experiencing significant growth driven by strong market demand and strategic execution. Headquarters in Virginia, and with operations in two countries and four states, they are expanding its operational footprint and infrastructure to support increasing volume and customer demand, while maintaining a focus on efficiency, environmental impact and long-term value creation. The

Opportunity:

Our client is seeking an accomplished recycling operations leader to lead a global recycling materials business unit through its next stage of growth, operational refinement, and strategic expansion. With autonomy, the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) will be responsible for the overall leadership, strategic direction and operational performance of the business. This role will oversee multiple warehouses with a focus on logistics, inventory management, reporting and environmental/health & safety compliance. Overseeing multiple recycling plants with a focus on delivering highly efficient, high quality operational performance, and development of a high performing culture.

This position will initially report to the President of the holding company during an onboarding period designed to build organizational knowledge, before transitioning to report directly to the Chairman of the Board, with two (2) direct reports.

The Requirements:
• 10–15+ years of relevant experience in an executive leadership role as a CEO, COO, President, VP of Operations, General Manager, or divisional leader with enterprise-level responsibility. Open to an executive who’s drive, ambition and hunger to win outweigh years of experience.
• Proven track record of driving growth, operational excellence, and strategic plan execution with P&L management, capital allocation and long -term business planning.
• Recycling and/or waste operations, environmental sectors are preferred. Must have a background in high volume/high velocity industries such as industrial, logistics, warehousing, and/or manufacturing while overseeing multi-state/multi-location.
• Proven experience leading businesses through scale-up, restructuring, or turnaround situations in a fast-paced environment. Proven expertise driving continuous improvement initiatives is key for this role. • Strong financial & operational acumen with the ability to drive strategic direction and align organization to successfully achieve goals/objectives.
• Experience with the following is a plus: operating in privately held, and/or sponsor-backed environments, M&A, integration, or international leadership. Experience working within large, complex organizations is strongly preferred. Knowledge of the metal production industry and market is a plus.
• High emotional intelligence with a proven ability to build respect, credibility, and trust from the BOD, senior management team, staff and external constituencies.
• Deep understanding of inventory management & security, safety, compliance, environmental responsibility with EHS a significant part, and risk management. Commercially astute, metric-driven & people focused.
• Excellent problem-solving, leadership, and decision-making skills with a collaborative demeanor. Executive presence, decisive and confident leader with strong business judgment.
• Exceptional communication, influence, and relationship-building skills with all levels of leadership. • Resilient, adaptable, and passionate about motivating others to excel through integrity, accountability, and hands-on leadership. • Commercially minded; ability to develop long-term strategic plans inclusive of capturing new business (sectors).

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer [IN PERSON/RELOCATION]
jobs.description:
Yutori Method™ is managing the recruitment for this role.

This is a full-time, in-person position based in Gold River, CA.

Candidates must be able to commute daily to the office/off-site events in the local area or be open to relocation.

About the Companies

This role supports the Principal's holding companies, a portfolio of businesses spanning real estate, lending, investments, and related ventures, with Optimus Capital serving as the largest and most established operating company.

While each business has its own focus, they are united by a shared vision for growth, operational excellence, and long-term value creation. As the portfolio continues to expand, the organization is investing in the leadership infrastructure needed to create greater alignment, scalability, and execution across the enterprise.

The Chief Operating Officer will partner directly with the Principal to provide operational leadership across the broader portfolio—ensuring priorities are aligned, execution remains coordinated, and each company benefits from a cohesive strategic direction while supporting the continued growth of the overall enterprise.

The Opportunity

This is an opportunity to become the CEO's closest strategic operating partner and the trusted translator between a highly visionary founder and the rest of the organization.

As Chief Operating Officer, you will serve as the operational counterpart to the CEO, helping transform ideas into clear priorities, coordinated execution, and measurable business outcomes. A significant part of your role will be understanding not only what the CEO is saying, but what he means—bringing clarity to complex ideas, ensuring teams are aligned around the broader vision, and amplifying his leadership throughout the organization.

You will act as the CEO's whisperer and organizational interpreter, creating the systems, operating rhythms, and accountability that allow the business to execute with confidence while preserving the speed and creativity that make the company successful.

This role is ideal for a leader who naturally sees both the big picture and the operational details—someone who thrives on creating alignment, building organizational clarity, developing leaders, and ensuring the entire company is moving in the same direction with purpose and momentum.

Why This Role Is Exciting

Partner directly with a visionary CEO as the organization's primary operational leader
Lead the execution and alignment of multiple business entities within a growing private lending platform
Build scalable systems, processes, and operating rhythms that support long-term growth
Drive meaningful business impact through KPI visibility, financial performance, and operational excellence
Shape the organization's leadership culture by creating accountability and clarity across teams
Leverage automation, AI, and modern operational tools to improve efficiency and decision-making
Join a company positioned for continued growth with the opportunity to influence its next stage of success

The Right Fit Candidate

This role is designed for someone who naturally becomes the operational counterpart to visionary leaders. You excel at listening beneath the surface, translating evolving ideas into organizational clarity, and ensuring teams understand not only what needs to happen, but why . You create focus, alignment, and accountability, allowing the CEO to remain visionary while the organization executes with confidence.

You are someone who:
Thrives partnering with visionary founders and translating ideas into execution
Not afraid of ambiguity and change of plans
Builds trust through consistency, sound judgment, and strong communication
Leads with humility while holding teams accountable for results
Makes decisions confidently using both data and business intuition
Enjoys improving systems, workflows, and organizational effectiveness
Brings a proactive, solutions-oriented mindset to operational challenges
Is comfortable managing multiple priorities across a complex organization
Embraces technology, automation, and AI as tools for scaling operations, and can bring demonstrated use cases for success
Wants to build a long-term partnership with a growing business
Understands marketing principles and has an eye for design

What You'll Do

CEO Enablement

Serve as the primary integrator between the CEO and the organization, translating vision into structured execution
Prioritize initiatives, manage executive follow-through, and ensure decisions move forward efficiently
Protect the CEO's time by removing operational bottlenecks and improving organizational alignment
Ensure communication, delegation, and strategic priorities are clearly defined across the business

Company Operations

Lead cross-functional execution across departments, teams, and business entities
Establish operating rhythms, accountability systems, and leadership meeting cadences
Improve workflows, eliminate bottlenecks, and implement automation and AI-enabled efficiencies
Oversee operational alignment across sales, lending, processing, compliance, and corporate functions

Planning & Business Performance

Lead quarterly and annual planning alongside executive leadership
Build KPI dashboards and reporting systems that provide real-time business visibility
Translate strategic objectives into measurable execution plans
Use operational and financial data to drive decision-making and continuous improvement

Revenue & Growth

Own operational accountability tied to revenue, profitability, and organizational performance
Partner with sales leadership to improve pipeline execution and operational throughput
Lead budgeting, forecasting, financial reporting, and departmental accountability
Connect operational performance with financial outcomes while reducing founder dependency

Team Leadership

Develop organizational clarity around ownership, priorities, and success metrics
Foster accountability through consistent follow-up and leadership alignment
Partner with department leaders to resolve operational friction and improve collaboration
Ensure strategic decisions are effectively communicated and implemented across the organization

Requirements

Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, Operations, or related field required (MBA preferred)
7–10+ years of operations leadership experience, preferably within private lending, mortgage, financial services, or real estate
Proven success serving as a COO, Integrator, or senior operations executive in a growing organization
Experience partnering directly with founders or visionary CEOs
Strong understanding of operational systems, KPI reporting, financial performance, and organizational scaling
Experience with Salesforce, Monday.com (or similar), reporting dashboards, automation platforms, and AI-enabled operational tools
Experience overseeing compliance, risk management, and operational governance
Must be authorized to work in the United States without sponsorship
Valid driver’s license
Russian language (A PLUS)

Work Environment & Compensation

Location: In-person, Gold River, CA in office or local off-site events (5 days/week)
Hours: Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM PST (flexibility for occasional evening events)
Expected Start Date: August 15, 2026
Salary: $200,000+ annually plus performance bonus
Relocation assistance available for the right candidate
Benefits: 401(k), Health, Dental, Vision, PTO after 90 days
Must be authorized to work in the U.S. without sponsorship
Reasonable accommodations available for qualified individuals with disabilities

jobs.title: Managing Director of Operational Strategy
jobs.description:
Follow your passion

Are you looking to make a BIG impact? At Zeta, we pursue an unprecedented combination of high academic achievement and social-emotional development for our students. We insist that every student receives a world-class education while fostering a love for learning. We are passionate about changing the landscape of public education in New York City and beyond, and we are uncompromising in our mission.

Currently, we operate multiple elementary and middle schools in the Bronx and Upper Manhattan, and are opening three new elementary schools in Queens for the 2025-26 school year
. We plan to grow and open 1-2 schools each year because children in every community deserve excellent, equitable education. Join our growing team today, and be a change agent for children.

Our Network office is located at 401 W 218th St., New York, NY 10034. Our Network team follows a hybrid work schedule: we work in-person from our office on Tuesdays and Thursdays with optional remote days on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays, if your team's scope of work permits.

Deliver powerful results

To achieve our vision, we are searching for a Managing
Director of Operational Strategy
with a Bachelor's Degree and 10+ years of work experience in management consulting, finance, scale-up of growing organizations, or strategic roles in high-performance organizations.
MBA is highly preferred, but not required. The position title will be commensurate with candidate experience.

Ideal candidates have a creative, problem-solving mindset, disciplined focus on scalable processes, and interest in education. We are looking for teammates with a strong analytical skillset ( e.g., ability to analyze large datasets and create models) and mastery of storytelling ( e.g., ability to write for multiple audiences and create effective decks). This role reports to the Chief Operating Officer, Network.

Responsibilities:

Provide strategic visioning and project management for critical initiatives to support the network's growth plan
Design and implement seamless, effective, and scalable operational processes across all areas of the organization that support our schools, including student outreach, talent recruitment, people operations, and organizational culture and training
Lead cross-functional teams in project planning and execution, ensuring that all departments contribute effectively to the strategic scaling plans
Champion a culture of continuous improvement by encouraging feedback and ideas from all levels of the organization, integrating diverse perspectives into strategic planning and scaling efforts
Identify areas of improvements across the organization and use data, experience, and best practices to provide creative solutions
Wear multiple hats as a part of a lean scale-up team, supporting the work of others on the team and taking on new projects and tasks as needed

Join our purpose-driven community

Zeta teammates are lifelong learners who live with child-like joy and a deep sense of purpose. We disrupt the status quo and tackle problems together. Our team stands united by our commitment to a limitless future for every child. Our Zeta team is comprised of top talent with incredible schooling and private sector experience. At Zeta:

We are passionate about our mission of providing world-class education to all students
We bring a mindset of growth, flexibility, and openness to feedback
We are hungry and determined to not only meet our goals, but surpass them
We are humble, willing to put the team's needs ahead of our own, and committed to contributing to a collaborative work environment
We have a high bar for excellence and sweat over the details
We are caring and put team over individual at all times

We are hiring for an immediate opening
.
The salary range for this position is between $125,000 and $175,000. New hires can expect to be brought into the organization at a salary at or near the start of the range, depending on relevant experience and internal equity. Compensation is based on our salary scales. This position is eligible to receive an annual discretionary, merit-based performance bonus.

How does Zeta invest in you?

We offer great benefits:

Comprehensive and affordable medical, dental, and vision plans
Flexible spending accounts for eligible medical purchases and commuter expenses
403(b) retirement plan; Zeta offers matching contributions toward your savings
Competitive compensation

We provide awesome perks:

Monthly stipend for wellness and lifestyle expenses (gym memberships, salon appointments, concerts – it's up to you!)
4 live therapy sessions per month through Talkspace and free Headspace subscription
MacBook Air and specified monthly reimbursement for use of a mobile phone
Fully stocked teacher workrooms with favorite snacks and Nespresso machine
Zeta swag, team outings, and NYC's finest sweet treats
Books for professional development

We care deeply about your personal and professional growth:

Clear pathway to professional growth
Consistent feedback and support from managers and teammates
Community Circle practice to build and strengthen our sense of team
Interactive Mindfulness and DEIJ programming

Apply Now!

Equity is at the heart of our mission. We have a deep commitment to pursuing diversity and striving for equity. Zeta is an equal opportunity employer. Candidates of all backgrounds are encouraged to apply. Work authorization is required (candidate must have permanent authorization to work in the U.S.).

jobs.title: President and Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
President & Chief Executive Officer

South Carolina Independent Colleges and Universities (SCICU)

Columbia, South Carolina

South Carolina Independent Colleges and Universities (SCICU) seeks an accomplished, visionary leader to serve as its next President & Chief Executive Officer.
Representing 20 independent colleges and universities across South Carolina, SCICU advocates for student access, educational choice, institutional excellence, and public policies that strengthen independent higher education.
The President & CEO serves as the chief advocate, strategist, spokesperson, fundraiser, and organizational leader for SCICU. Reporting to the Board of Trustees, the President works closely with college and university presidents, legislators, state leaders, donors, business partners, and other stakeholders to advance the interests of South Carolina's independent higher education sector.
Key Responsibilities

Legislative Advocacy & Public Policy

Lead SCICU's legislative and public policy agenda.
Advocate for state financial aid programs that support student access and affordability.
Build and maintain strong relationships with state and federal policymakers.
Monitor and respond to legislative, regulatory, and budget developments affecting member institutions.

Strategic Leadership

Develop and communicate a compelling vision for SCICU.
Advance initiatives that strengthen member institutions and enhance the visibility of independent higher education.
Identify opportunities to expand services and support for member campuses.

Member Relations

Maintain strong relationships with the presidents of SCICU's 20 member institutions.
Build consensus among institutions with diverse missions and backgrounds.
Serve as a trusted advisor and resource on policy, advocacy, and strategic issues.

Fundraising & External Relations

Lead fundraising efforts and cultivate relationships with foundations, corporations, and individual donors.
Strengthen partnerships that support scholarships, grants, and other SCICU initiatives.
Serve as the public voice for independent higher education in South Carolina.

Organizational Leadership

Lead and develop a high-performing staff team.
Ensure sound financial management, operational excellence, and organizational effectiveness.
Work closely with the Board of Trustees to establish priorities and measure success.

Desired Qualifications

Significant leadership experience in higher education, public policy, government relations, nonprofit leadership, association management, or a related field.
Demonstrated success working with elected officials, policymakers, boards, and senior leaders.
Strong understanding of higher education policy, student financial aid, and institutional advocacy.
Exceptional communication, relationship-building, and consensus-building skills.
Fundraising and donor relations experience preferred.
Advanced degree preferred.

Why This Opportunity?

This is a unique opportunity to shape the future of independent higher education in South Carolina. The successful candidate will champion student choice, strengthen access to higher education, advocate for critical student aid programs, and serve as the leading voice for South Carolina's independent colleges and universities.
To Apply

Confidential nominations, inquiries, and application materials should be submitted electronically to:
presidentialsearch@scicu.org

Application materials should include:
Cover letter
Current résumé or curriculum vitae
Contact information for five professional references

References will not be contacted without the candidate's permission.
Review of applications will begin immediately and continue until the position is filled.

jobs.title: Executive Director, Quality Management (H)
jobs.description:
Current Employees:

If you are a current Staff, Faculty or Temporary employee at the University of Miami, please click here to log in to Workday to use the internal application process. To learn how to apply for a faculty or staff position, please review this tip sheet.

The Executive Director, Quality Management collaborates in the strategic development and implementation of sustainable and effective clinical quality system management, programs, and initiatives in response to the changing needs of the University. This position is responsible for the implementation of improvement programs by using evidence based protocols and clinical care pathways that enhance safe, cost effective processes in the Hospital and/or Ambulatory Divisions. Contributes to areas that need to redesign processes and/or maintain processes in order to meet the continual changes of accrediting agencies, federal and state regulatory agencies and evidence-based practice.

Leads assigned quality system management operations for the enterprise, and responsible for the assigned unit’s deliverables. Provides direction and expert advice in the development and maintenance of clinical quality systems, programs, and initiatives that adhere to all legal and regulatory requirements and standards. Facilitates development of division-wide knowledge and education of key quality indicators, metrics, benchmark data, and reporting systems and tools. In collaboration with facilities, ensures development and implementation of appropriate accountability and reporting and processes within facilities. Contributes to the attainment of business goals by positively impacting continuous quality improvement outcomes. Optimizes use of resources, equipment and services, ensuring a high level of services. Actively seeks ways to contain costs and reduce unwarranted variation in care without compromising patient safety, quality of care, or the services delivered. Leads and makes decisions for effective implementation of division-wise strategies, programs, and initiatives related to clinical quality improvement to promote continuous readiness by assisting healthcare and medical staff on performance improvement on national, state, and locally reported measures, as well as compliance with quality national programs. Performs supervisory responsibilities in accordance with the University’s policies and applicable law. Responsible for interviewing, hiring, training employees; planning, assigning, and directing work; appraising performance; rewarding and disciplining employees; addressing and resolving problems. Leads initiatives to pursue the elimination of preventable harm by implementing high reliability organization techniques. Recognizes patterns, trends, and identify initiatives that impact quality improvement for the enterprise. Continuously monitors and evaluates quality outcomes and appropriateness of patient care throughout the division; identifying opportunities to improve outcomes. Alerts CPSQO if any identified outcome variances and assists in facilitating division response to quality issues, and incident reporting management. Establishes and continuously assesses the effectiveness of the internal controls within the unit and compliance with University policies and procedures. Ensures employees are trained on controls within the function and on University policy and procedures.

This list of duties and responsibilities is not intended to be all-inclusive and may be expanded to include other duties or responsibilities as necessary.

Qualifications

CORE QUALIFICATIONS

Education:

Bachelor’s Degree in relevant field

Certification and Licensing:

Not Applicable

Required Experience:

Minimum 7 years of relevant experience. Clinical experience is required. Experienced with JCAHO and ACHA standards is required.

Knowledge, Skills and Behaviors:

Ability to maintain effective interpersonal relationships
Ability to communicate effectively in both oral and written form
Skill in collecting, organizing and analyzing data
Proficiency in computer software (i.e., Microsoft Office)
Knowledge of business and management principles
Ability to effectively plan, delegate and/or supervise the work of others.
Ability to lead, motivate, develop and train others.

DEPARTMENT ADDENDUM

Department Specific Functions

Leads the overall hospital accreditation and regulatory survey readiness programs, and continuous quality assurance in either a primary leadership role, or as a secondary leadership support role. Assumes primary liaison between The Joint Commission (TJC) and UMHC on accreditation-related processes including but not limited to development of facility responses to survey findings, quality-of-care concerns, application submission, on-site visit and post-survey activity proceedings. Presides as Chairperson of the Accreditation Readiness & Response Committee (AR2C) and provides quarterly report to the Patient Safety Events Committee unto the UHealth Board of Directors. Identifies trends, gaps, and proactively develops programs with variety of constituents including hospital leadership, medical and nursing staff to improve organizational performance and compliance with accreditation and regulatory standards that impact quality and safety improvement for UMHC. Supports initiatives in pursuit of elimination of preventable harm by advising high reliability organization techniques. Drives short-term strategies that have long-term operational excellence impact on the achievement and sustainment of accreditation, program certifications and licensure. Translates accreditation, regulatory, and compliance strategies and procedures into actionable plans. Collaborates and advises senior leadership of outcome measures, matrix, performance improvement and effectiveness in alignment with UMHC and UHealth System Mission and Vision. Validates UMHC policies and practices through surveillance reviews for alignment and compliance with accreditation and regulatory standards. Presents survey readiness and compliance reports to UMHC leadership via a defined, formal reporting structure. Educates faculty, administration and staff relative to accreditation survey readiness; performance improvement; and, methods of collecting, analyzing, and reporting of data. Maintains current with accreditation and regulatory changes, evidence-based care practice guidelines and outcomes, and evolving professional and social issues in health care. Optimizes use of resources, equipment and services, ensuring a high level of services. Actively seeks ways to contain costs and reduce unwarranted variations without compromising safety, quality or services delivered. Verifies internal control compliance oversight of assets, policies and procedures, reliability of internal and external reporting, and efficiency and effectiveness of operations. Creates an effective control environment, conducts risk assessment, implements and monitors controls as appropriate. Oversees the department financial management, planning, operations, systems, and controls. Performs supervisory responsibilities in accordance with the University’s policies and applicable law. Responsible for interviewing, hiring, training employees; planning, assigning, and directing work; appraising performance; rewarding and disciplining employees; addressing and resolving problems. Other duties as assigned.

The University of Miami offers competitive salaries and a comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, tuition remission and more.

UHealth-University of Miami Health System, South Florida's only university-based health system, provides leading-edge patient care powered by the ground breaking research and medical education at the Miller School of Medicine. As an academic medical center, we are proud to serve South Florida, Latin America and the Caribbean. Our physicians represent more than 100 specialties and sub-specialties, and have more than one million patient encounters each year. Our tradition of excellence has earned worldwide recognition for outstanding teaching, research and patient care. We're the challenge you've been looking for.

The University of Miami is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Applicants and employees are protected from discrimination based on certain categories protected by Federal law.

jobs.title: Director of Philanthropy
jobs.description:
Position Details

Get to Know Us!

Marshall University is a public comprehensive university with a rich history as one of the oldest institutions of higher learning in West Virginia. Founded in 1837 and named after Chief Justice John Marshall, definer of the Constitution, Marshall University advances the public good through innovative, accredited educational programs. We are located in downtown Huntington, West Virginia, with additional locations in South Charleston, Point Pleasant, Teays Valley and Beckley. Marshall University’s mission, inspired by our Vision and Creed, includes a commitment to:

Offer a wide range of high quality, affordable, and accessible undergraduate, graduate, and professional education that prepares students to think, learn, work, and live in an evolving global society.
Create opportunities and experiences to foster understanding and appreciation of the rich diversity of thought and culture.
Maintain a dynamic intellectual, artistic, and cultural life by promoting and supporting research and creative activities by undergraduates, graduates, and faculty.
Contribute to the quality of life of the community, region, and beyond through applied research, economic development, health care, and cultural enrichment.
Cultivate the development of an inclusive, just, and equitable community.

Come Meet Marshall and discover what it means to be part of the Marshall University family!

Come join our Herd!

We are committed to attracting and retaining quality faculty and staff. In doing so, Marshall University offers outstanding benefit packages to eligible employees only. (E-Class in the job posting). Marshall University Research Corporation (MURC) employees have different benefit options than those listed below.

Work week is 37.5 hrs.
A great highlight in December is that Marshall University employees receive almost two weeks off for Winter Break!
Holidays – Employees receive 13 Paid Holidays
Annual and Sick Leave – Classified Staff, Non-Classified Staff and 12-month Faculty earn up to 24 days of annual leave per year (max 360 hours) and up to 18 days of sick leave per year. ) Employee Leave
Health Insurance – Marshall University offers health insurance through West Virginia Public Employees Insurance Agency (PEIA). There are seven (7) health benefit plans.
Educational/Tuition Benefits – Marshall University provides tuition benefits to regular employees (after probationary period) and their dependent children.
Retirement Plans – Marshall University offers retirement through Teachers Insurance and Annuity Association (TIAA). Full-time benefits eligible employees must participate in the mandated retirement plan (401(a)).
Life Insurance – Basic term life insurance ($10,000) at no cost and may purchase optional life insurance coverage with pre-tax earnings.
Wellness Programs – PEIA provides various care management programs and the Pathways to Wellness program.

                            We are . . . Marshall!

Position Information

Position Title Director of Philanthropy Banner Position Number 02132026 E-Class TN - MU Non-employee Position (NOT Benefits Eligible) Department Sr VP Development - MU6000 Job Description

Please note: This is a Marshall University Foundation, Inc benefits eligible position and not a Marshall University state position.

The Director of Philanthropy is a key member of the Development Team and responsible for establishing, building and deepening meaningful relationships with Marshall University Foundation prospects and donors. Directors of Philanthropy cultivate, solicit and steward prospects and donors with the capacity to make major gifts at the $25,000 level and greater.

This is a position for an entrepreneurial and relationship-driven fundraiser to expand and deepen the philanthropic contributions of a portfolio of prospects and donors. In 2026, the Marshall University Foundation will launch a major fundraising campaign, and the Director of Philanthropy will work collaboratively with Foundation and University colleagues to fulfill the campaign goals.

Strategic Prospect and Donor Engagement

Work closely with the CDO and Foundation and Development staff, university deans, faculty and key volunteers to pursue a coordinated and strategic program of cultivation, solicitation and stewardship of donors.
Identify, qualify, and advance major gift prospects through proactive prospecting, referrals, research, events, and relationship-building.
Make direct solicitations and close gifts; maintain a high level of donor and prospect engagement, including regular in-person and virtual meetings
Manage a major gift donor portfolio of current, dormant, and prospective donors with the capacity to make gifts of $25,000 or greater.
By building relationships through cultivation and stewardship raise a minimum of $500,000 annually.

Portfolio Management

Leverage prospect research, data, and analytics to prioritize outreach, refine strategy, and strengthen the donor pipeline.
Document contacts and strategies using the moves management system.
Adhere to the Foundation’s policies and procedures for accepting, recording and acknowledging gifts and pledges.

Other Duties

Pursue professional growth activities in collaboration with the Chief Development Officer.
Participate as a full member of the development team attending prospect management meetings, professional development activities and other staff meetings, as appropriate.
Monitor and process expenses on a timely basis and within approved budgets.
Perform other duties as assigned.

Salary Range Salary is commensurate upon education, experience and internal equity Salary Type Salary Time Type Full-Time Work Location OTHR - Other Off-Campus Location

Qualifications

Must be able to perform all essential job duties as outlined in the job description.

Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree from an accredited college or university.

Experience

Minimum of 3 years of progressive and successful philanthropic development, relationship-based institutional sales, marketing, and/or related experience.
Proven experience developing and managing relationships with high-level prospects.
Excellent interpersonal and communication skills – written, verbal, and presentation.
Strong organizational skills, with attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple priorities.
Ability to travel locally and nationally to fulfill Key Responsibilities.
Ability to work evenings and weekends to fulfill Key Responsibilities.

jobs.title: Business Development Director
jobs.description:
The Houma-Terrebonne Airport Commission is seeking a Business Development Director. Reporting to the Executive Director, the Business Development Director provides strategic leadership for business development, economic development, and commercial growth initiatives for the Houma-Terrebonne Airport Commission (HTAC). This role is responsible for identifying and advancing opportunities that enhance airport utilization, attract aviation and aerospace-related investment, and strengthen long-term revenue generation, with particular emphasis on advanced air mobility (AAM) and unmanned aircraft systems (UAS). The Director serves as a key representative of HTAC within the aviation, advanced air mobility, unmanned aircraft systems, aerospace, and economic development communities while fostering partnerships with public agencies, private industry, and regional stakeholders. The position plays a central role in expanding the airport’s market presence and supporting the organization’s long-term strategic growth objectives.

Key responsibilities include:
Lead business development initiatives focused on aviation, aerospace, logistics, (AAM), and (UAS) opportunities
(AAM) and (UAS) initiatives, including the recruitment of operators, the development of testing and operational infrastructure, and the pursuit of partnerships that position HUM as a regional hub for emerging aviation technologies
Develop and maintain strategic partnerships with industry leaders, government agencies, and economic development organizations
Identify and pursue new revenue-generating opportunities involving aeronautical and nonaeronautical development
Represent HTAC at industry conferences, trade shows, and stakeholder engagement events
Negotiate partnership agreements and support leases, contracts, and memorandums of understanding
Coordinate site visits, presentations, and outreach efforts for prospective business partners and investors
Support grant pursuits, public-private partnerships, and funding initiatives tied to airport development
Conduct market research and industry analysis to identify emerging trends and competitive opportunities
Prepare executive briefings, strategic recommendations, and presentations for airport leadership and commissioners

Preferred Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in business, aviation management, public administration, or a related field; equivalent experience may be considered
Seven years of progressively responsible experience in business development, economic development, or aviation-related industries
Knowledge of aviation, aerospace, transportation, (AAM), or (UAS) sectors
Experience developing strategic partnerships, negotiating agreements, and managing complex initiatives
Familiarity with airport operations, FAA regulations, and aviation infrastructure development
Strong communication, presentation, analytical, and stakeholder engagement skills

Salary & Benefits

The position offers a competitive compensation package with a salary range of $122,500 to $158,500, commensurate on qualifications and experience.
